name: bed
title: bed
description:
A network protocol fuzzer
BED is a program which is designed to check daemons
for potential buffer overflows, format strings et. al.

commands.name: bed
commands.description: BED 0.5 by mjm ( www.codito.de ) & eric ( www.snake-basket.de )

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bed because A network protocol fuzzer.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bed as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bed [options] [args]` as documented upstream for bed.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bed with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bed --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
